content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Changed verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success to fall back to legacy mappings (run_id: None) when no current-run mappings exist and reconstruction finds nothing. The initial filter now uses .iter().cloned() instead of .into_iter() to preserve the mappings Vec for the fallback path. After reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings returns, if both all_bead_mappings and reconstructed are empty, the function collects legacy mappings (run_id: None, active_bead_id == bead_id) and uses those instead. Legacy mappings with completion_round: None bypass the round-filtering logic naturally (effective_max_round is None â†’ all legacy mappings pass). Amendment 2: Added bidirectional bead ID alias resolution in verify_bead_exists. The existing canonicalâ†’short retry (strip milestone prefix, e.g. '9ni.8.5.3' â†’ '8.5.3') was augmented with a shortâ†’canonical direction that prepends the milestone prefix (e.g. '8.5.3' â†’ '9ni.8.5.3'). Added milestone_prefix_of() helper in task_prompt_contract.rs that extracts the prefix before the first dot. verify_bead_exists now accepts an optional milestone_prefix parameter; both call sites (reconcile_planned_elsewhere and verify_mappings) extract the prefix from active_bead_id. The shortâ†’canonical expansion only fires when the bead_id doesn't already carry the prefix, avoiding redundant br show calls. Amendment 3: Hardened sentinel write failure handling in engine.rs by adding a single retry via .or_else() before logging. The first failure logs tracing::warn and immediately retries record_planned_elsewhere_round_sentinel with a fresh timestamp. Only if the retry also fails does it log to the runtime log â€” now at LogLevel::Error instead of Warn â€” with 'after retry' in the message for operator visibility. The existing aggregate-based authoritative_max_round in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success already compensates for missing sentinels in the success reconciliation path, so the retry addresses the transient failure window for the journal-based path in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Changed verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success to use .iter().cloned() instead of .into_iter() for the initial current-run filter, preserving the mappings Vec. After reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings, added fallback: when all_bead_mappings and reconstructed are both empty, collects legacy mappings (run_id: None) for the same bead_id.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2a: Added milestone_prefix_of() public helper in task_prompt_contract.rs â€” extracts the prefix before the first dot from a canonical bead ID (e.g. '9ni' from '9ni.8.5.3'). Returns None if no dot or empty prefix. Added milestone_prefix_of_extracts_prefix test.
3. [Completed] Amendment 2b: Added milestone_prefix: Option<&str> parameter to verify_bead_exists in planned_elsewhere.rs. After the existing canonicalâ†’short retry (Direction 1), added Direction 2: shortâ†’canonical expansion by prepending milestone_prefix + '.' when the bead_id doesn't already carry that prefix. Only fires on Stale result.
4. [Completed] Amendment 2c: Updated both call sites of verify_bead_exists â€” reconcile_planned_elsewhere extracts prefix from input.active_bead_id via milestone_prefix_of, verify_mappings extracts from active_bead_id parameter. Both pass the prefix to verify_bead_exists.
5. [Completed] Amendment 2d: Added short_to_canonical_alias_resolves_bead test that verifies the 3-attempt resolution chain (8.5.3 not found â†’ 5.3 not found â†’ 9ni.8.5.3 succeeds) using MockBrRunner with 3 queued responses.
6. [Completed] Amendment 3: Replaced single-attempt sentinel write in engine.rs with .or_else() retry pattern. First failure logs tracing::warn and retries. Only permanent failure (both attempts) logs LogLevel::Error to runtime log with 'after retry' in message.
7. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix three minor formatting issues (collapsed multi-line let prefix bindings and expanded assert! macro).
8. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (693 lib, 174 integration, 1 tmux, 758 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (success_reconciliation.rs, planned_elsewhere.rs, task_prompt_contract.rs, engine.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success at success_reconciliation.rs:720-749 now uses .iter().cloned() for initial filter, then falls back to legacy mappings (run_id: None) when both all_bead_mappings and reconstructed are empty. Legacy mappings with completion_round: None bypass the effective_max_round filter at lines 758-771 since max is None â†’ all mappings pass.
- Amendment 2 verified: milestone_prefix_of at task_prompt_contract.rs extracts prefix correctly (test confirms '9ni.8.5.3' â†’ Some('9ni'), 'no-dots' â†’ None). verify_bead_exists at planned_elsewhere.rs:78-116 now tries 3 directions: original ID, canonicalâ†’short strip, shortâ†’canonical expansion. short_to_canonical_alias_resolves_bead test confirms full resolution chain with MockBrRunner.
- Amendment 3 verified: engine.rs sentinel write at lines 7617-7651 uses .or_else() retry with tracing::warn on first failure. Permanent failure logs LogLevel::Error with 'after retry' message. The aggregate-based authoritative_max_round at success_reconciliation.rs:758 provides defense-in-depth for the reconciliation path.
- git diff --stat HEAD -- src/: 4 files changed â€” success_reconciliation.rs (legacy fallback), planned_elsewhere.rs (bidirectional alias, new test), task_prompt_contract.rs (milestone_prefix_of, new test), engine.rs (sentinel retry)
- Files modified: src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs, src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The legacy fallback in Amendment 1 only activates when no current-run mappings AND no reconstructed mappings exist. If a bead has any current-run mappings (even from a different round), legacy mappings are still excluded. This is correct â€” the presence of current-run data means the run_id tracking is active for this bead.
- The shortâ†’canonical expansion in Amendment 2 assumes the milestone prefix can be extracted from active_bead_id. If active_bead_id has no dot (unusual), milestone_prefix_of returns None and the expansion is skipped. The canonicalâ†’short direction still fires in this case.
- The sentinel retry in Amendment 3 adds one additional journal append on transient failure. If the failure is caused by a full disk or permissions issue, the retry will also fail immediately. The Error-level log ensures operator visibility for persistent failures.
